reasoning: The DGS6MO series shows a clear downward trend over the past 3 months, falling from 4.31% in July 2025 to 3.76% by Oct 24, 2025. This signals that short-term rates are dropping as part of the Fed's rate cut cycle. Since short-term yields anticipate Fed moves, the 6-month yield will likely continue to face downward pressure as more cuts are priced in or materialize over November 2025 through January 2026. Because probabilities must push toward 50% for longer horizons but the macro trend for rate cuts is very strong over year-end, I expect continued gradual declines with standard daily volatility, giving probabilities of 40%, 35%, and 30% that the yield will be higher on the respective resolution dates compared to late October.
